event: Allegorical assembly of the great English poets
symbols: central enthroned figure — personification of English Poetry or the Muse presiding over the literary tradition
gothic arches — the cathedral-like sanctity of artistic tradition and national heritage
laurel wreaths — poetic achievement and immortal fame
medieval and classical costumes — the span of English literary history from Chaucer to the Romantics
book and scroll — the written word as vessel of enduring truth
kneeling and standing figures — varying degrees of homage and participation in the poetic tradition
green robed figure — possible embodiment of Nature or pastoral poetry
description: A large allegorical composition set within a pair of ornate gothic arches. A central figure stands elevated on a platform or throne, draped in white or light robes, presiding over a diverse crowd of historical and literary figures. Figures in medieval, Elizabethan, and later period costumes gather around, some kneeling, some standing, some gesturing. At the lower center, a robed green figure sits prominently. The arches are decorated with carved portraits in medallions. The distant background reveals a pale sky and landscape.

_meta.title: The Seeds and Fruit of English Poetry
